Looks like this patch breaks the setting of lov parameters.

 Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mkdir -p /mnt/lustre-mds1; mount -t lustre lustre-mdt1/mdt1 /mnt/lustre-mds1
 Lustre:    cmd=cf00f 0:lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov  1:lov.stripecount=-1  
 LustreError: 15b-f: MGC10.9.4.170@tcp: The configuration from log 'lustre-MDT0000' failed from the MGS (-22).

Patch Set 2:

Sorry I haven't got into looking into this. Will shortly.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

This looks like it is missing a test for stripecount = -1.  The original patch should never have landed if it broke that setting.

Patch Set 8:

Looking at the logs it was broken with commit 860e20e42ddc4 so its been broke for awhile. Since lov "stripe_count" was used instead of lod "stripe_count" on the MDT it was missed. This change exposed that bug but now it is fixed. 

I has to say it is strange that stripe_count = -1 is missing from testing. Also if lod stripe_count is changed does it transmit to the clients to notify the lov layer? Something to look into and test. I can do a follow on patch for testing the -1 stripe count and lov layer notification.

parents.subject: LU-10965 doc: add mirror options to "lfs find" in lfs.1

commit.subject: LU-8066 lod: replace class_process_proc_param()
commit.message:
LU-8066 lod: replace class_process_proc_param()

With the move to sysfs for lod every lctl conf_param option will
send out a udev event. Replace class_process_proc_param() with
class_modify_config() so sysfs attributes can be set directly.

This patch exposed an error in stripecount_store() which doesn't
allow setting stripe count to -1 which is wrong.
